item.title: git 不是历史的终结 | git is not the end of history
item.description:
我特此愚蠢地预测，在 5 年内，我们将看到一个新的版本控制工具，我们称之为hju ，它将吸引数百万开发人员，承诺可能是“ hju就像 git，但更简单” .

具体来说：

  • 将您的 GitHub 存储库导入hju 只需单击一下。 在 git 仓库中转换你的hju仓库也很简单。
  • 可能是这里的主要障碍。
  • 更复杂的工作流程将留在 git 中。 特别是像 Linux 内核这样的项目，完全去中心化的集市项目，将看不到迁移到 hju 的意义。
  • 在很长一段时间内，git 可能会比 hju 拥有更多的用户。 但是有足够多的开发商认为拥有例如 20% 的市场份额是完全可持续的。
  • 另一方面， hju将专注于版本控制的最常见用法。 它将为可用性而设计。 用 hju 搞砸事情要比用 git 难得多。
  • 当提交 pull 请求时，你会觉得你更专注于手头的任务，而不是你的控制版本工具对你的期望。
  • 它会推动这样的想法，即如果 PR 中的 hju 历史看起来不像应该发生的事情，而是像实际发生的那样，那也没关系。 它将鼓励人们将小的 PR 集中在一件事情上并且可以被压缩（压缩将是默认设置）。
  • 在某些时候，训练营和大学课程会教授 hju 而不是 git，因为学习的概念与花费的时间之比要好得多。 如果您已经了解 hju，那么互联网上会有关于如何学习 git 的很棒的教程。
  • 最佳情况：微软已经有一个类似于 hju 的秘密内部项目，并将确保 GitHub 可以将其作为你使用git还是hju的实现细节。

    亲爱的读者，我的预测中最好的和最蹩脚的部分是什么？
    既然您也可以预测未来，那么您如何看待版本控制的未来？

一旦暂停，jmfayard 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，jmfayard 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

如果 jmfayard 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

原文:

I hereby am foolish enough to predict that in 5 years for now, we will see a new tool for version control, let's call it hju, that will attract millions of developers on a promise that could be "hju is like git, but simpler".

Specifically:

  • hju concepts will be a lot like git, you will have commits, branches, sha1, clones, ...
  • hju will have great interoperability with git. Importing your GitHub repository into hju will be one click. Transforming you hju repository in a git repo will also be simple.
  • hju will struggle to find a business model that supports a team strong enough to build a tool concurrent to git. Probably the main hurdle right here.
  • hju will not try to have feature parity with git. git can support all the complex workflows you have not imagined yet. The more complex workflow will stay with git. Especially projects like the Linux Kernel, bazaar projects that are completely decentralized, will see no point in migrating to hju.
  • hju will instead work hard to make the life simpler for the vast majority of projects where a small number of developers work together, know each other, trust each other.
  • git will probably have more users than hju for a long while. But there are enough developers that having for example 20% of the market is completely sustainable.
  • hju on the other hand will be focused on the most common usages of version control. It will be designed for usability. It will be significantly harder to fuck up things with hju than with git.
  • hju will lower the cognitive load. When submitting a pulll request, you will feel that you are more focused on the task at hand rather than on what your control version tool expects from you.
  • hju will discourage practices like rewriting history that makes it so enjoyable to shoot yourself in the foot. It will push the idea that it's OK if the hju history inside a PR doesn't look like what should have happened, but instead like what actually happened. It will encourage people to have small PRs focused on one thing and that can be squashed (squashing will be the default).
  • hju will have a vastly better learning curve than git. At some points bootcamps and university courses will teach hju rather than git because the ratio of concepts learned over time spent is so much better. There will be great tutorials on the internet on how to learn git if you already know hju.
  • hju will just work on Windows and Android

Best case scenario: Microsoft has already a secret internal project similar to hju and will make sure GitHub can make it an implementation detail of whether you use git or hju.

Dear readers, what's the best and the lamest part in my prediction?
Since you too can predict the future, how do you see the future of version control?

item.title: 非常感谢我们所有的模组！ 🙌 🙌 | Big Thanks to All Our Mods! 🙌
item.description:
嘿伙计们！

今天是美国感恩节，公司在 Forem（DEV 和CodeNewbie背后的人）放假。 大部分团队今天都休息，但我只在线几个小时来检查我们的滥用报告，驱逐垃圾邮件发送者😈，并为需要它的人提供支持。

幸运的是，我有很多帮助！ 我们的可信用户、标签版主和各种社区成员都有助于报告垃圾邮件、滥用行为和剽窃。 我们的版主（受信任的用户和标签模组）协助我（和我的合作伙伴） 犯罪良好的氛围， @caroline 🙌 ）每天通过报告行为准则 -违反行为，对低质量内容进行投票，并根据需要更正帖子上的标签，以便为每个人更好地组织网站。

当我今天早上浏览一堆报告时，我看到了这么多熟悉的用户名，并提醒我们的模组是多么慷慨地投入他们的时间来使 DEV 成为一个善良、安全、包容、有组织和无垃圾邮件的- 可以的地方。 我们的 mods 做这项工作纯粹是出于对 DEV 的热爱——没有报酬，也没有 heckuva lotta 认可。 相反，这些无名英雄大多在幕后行动，出于善意和希望建立一个我们都可以享受并从中受益的积极、乐于助人的社区。 我无法表达我对我们的主持人团队的感激之情！ 所以相反，这里有一组笨拙的“谢谢”GIF 来表达我的感激之情。

如果你想说声谢谢但不知道怎么说，请随意拿一张感恩的 GIF并将其放在评论中。

李🤖☀️🌊🛸👾👨‍💻- 独角兽开发商 - 一旦暂停，devteam 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，devteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，devteam 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 devteam 的操作：

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging devteam will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning devteam 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Heyo folks!

It's US Thanksgiving today and a company holiday here at Forem (the folks behind DEV & CodeNewbie). Much of the team is off for the day, but I'm online for just a couple hours to check our abuse reports, banish the spammers 😈, and offer support to those who need it.

Luckily, I have lotsa help! Our Trusted Users, Tag Moderators, and various community members all help to report spam, abusive behavior, and plagiarism. Our moderators (trusted users and tag mods) assist me (and my partner in crime good vibes, @caroline 🙌) on a daily basis by reporting Code of Conduct-violating behavior, downvoting low quality content, and correcting tags on posts as needed so that the site is better organized for everybody.

As I sift through the pile of reports this morning, I'm seeing so many familiar usernames and reminded how generous our mods are to dedicate their time toward making DEV a kind, safe, inclusive, organized, and as-spam-free-as-can-be place. Our mods do this work purely out of love for DEV — there is no pay and not a heckuva lotta recognition either. Rather, these unsung heroes act mostly behind-the-scenes, motivated by good intentions and a desire to foster a positive, helpful community that we can all enjoy and benefit from. 💚

I cannot say enough how grateful I feel for our moderator team! So instead, here's a collection of awkward "thank you" GIFs to show my appreciation.

If you wanna say thanks but don't know how, feel free to grab a thankful GIF and drop it in the comments.

Lee 🤖☀️🌊🛸👾👨‍💻 - Aug 1

Unicorn Developer - Jul 18

item.title: 完美的开源软件并不存在 | The perfect open-sourcer does not exist
item.description:
在过去的四个月里，我设定了一些目标来成为更好的开源者。
他们看起来像这样：

  • 年轻和在开源社区的经验较少，让所有这些看起来遥不可及。 尽管如此，我还是执行了它们。 无论您的目标是什么，我都希望您经历一次突破。

    在这个过程中，我清楚地认识到要将我的承诺付诸于我想要实现的目标。 我渴望在所做的事情上做到完美，但具有讽刺意味的是，这让我的工作延期了，最终我最终没有做。 但是，一旦我实现了它们，我想知道为什么我没有早点完成它们。

    谢谢你的想法，实现你的目标更容易，但要完美地实现目标却并非如此。

    贡献小到大🐛🗻

    为开源项目做出贡献并在社区中建立影响力

    无论您贡献的是小块代码还是大块代码，保持它们的一致性至关重要。 对特定项目的小贡献可以帮助您首先熟悉它并导致更大的事情。
    看看我对以下项目提出的一些拉取请求； 拥有无限力量的 palpatine 静态网站生成器！⚡

    对项目做出承诺并建立维护者的信誉

    我喜欢开发的个人项目之一是palpatine 。 我在这里写了关于它的博客，并且我不断地向它添加新功能！ 到目前为止，它已经达到5 次启动，并在开源社区的帮助下不断增长。

    我相信，保持良好的项目维护和营销是开源的一个非常重要的部分。 像书一样阅读代码📖🧙

    进行有意义的代码审查并回答问题

    几乎每个人似乎都很难审查别人的代码。 我的经历并没有太大不同，我也发现它完全具有挑战性。 如此之多，以至于我推迟了最长的时间来写这个博客。 然而，我明白，能够审查其他人的代码是一项重要的品质，也是一项很难获得的技能。 能够查看代码并提供有关代码的建设性反馈需要时间和实践，这样才能对项目和开发人员有所帮助。

    我复习了我两个同学的代码；

    回顾2️⃣

    回顾2️⃣

    我还不是代码审查方面的专家，没有写太多我想尽快单击“ Submit review按钮。 作为一名开发人员，您可能会说，有时您可能会发现其他开发人员比您更有经验，并且不想审查他们的代码。 然而，在这种情况下，审查质量好的或质量差的代码可以极大地提高您作为开发人员的水平。 一路上，我发现自己在寻找和学习很多我不知道的事情。
    这个资源是帮助我做出有影响力的评论的最重要的部分。

    更好的开源播放器👾

    在完成我上面计算的所有目标后，具有讽刺意味的是，再次完成这些目标感觉更容易。 在我尝试其中任何一个之前，它是非常压倒性的，它们似乎都超出了我的能力范围。 不要等到你的工作变得完美，然后开始做你真正喜欢的事情。
    成就感是如此令人满足，以至于您可以说； "you could do it once again." “你可以再做一次。”



原文:

Over the last four months, I have set some goals to become a better open-sourcer.
They looked something like this:

  • [x] Contribute to an open-source project and build influence in the community
  • [x] Make a commitment to a project and build up credibility as a maintainer
  • [x] Do a meaningful code review and answer issues

Being young and having less experience in open source community, made all of these seem out of reach. Nevertheless, I carried them out. Regardless of what goals you might have, I want you to go through your version of a breakthrough.

During the process, what became clear to me was to put my commitment to the goals I wanted to achieve. I have this desire to be perfect in things I do, but ironically this leaves my work overdue and ultimately I end up not doing them. Though, once I achieve them, I wonder why I haven't done them earlier.

It's easier to achieve your goals thank you think, but being perfect about them is not.

Contribute small to big 🐛🗻

Contribute to an open-source project and build influence in the community

Whether you contribute small or big chunks of code, being consistent about them carries vital importance. Small contributions to a particular project help you to get familiar with it at first and leads to something bigger.
Take a look at some pull requests I have raised to the following projects; withfig, cve-bin-tool, my-photohub, pr-approve-generator.

palpatine static site generator with unlimited power!⚡

Make a commitment to a project and build up credibility as a maintainer

One of the personal projects I love developing is palpatine. I blogged about it here and I am constantly adding new features to it! So far it has reached 5 starts and keeps growing with the help of the open-source community.

Keeping good maintenance on and marketing your projects is, I believe, a very significant part of open source. There are so many developers all around the world on GitHub potentially finding your project worthwhile.

Read Code Like A Book 📖 🧙

Do a meaningful code review and answer issues

Almost everyone seems to have had a hard time reviewing other people's codes. My experience was not very different, I too found it utterly challenging. So much so that, I was putting off writing this blog for the longest time. I understand, however, that being able to review other people's code is an important trait to have and a difficult skill to attain. It takes time and practice to be able to look at code and give constructive feedback about them so that it helps the project and the developer.

I reviewed the codes of two of my classmates from;

Review 2️⃣

Review 2️⃣

I am no expert at code reviews yet, without writing much I want to click on the Submit review button as soon as I can. You as a developer may relate, there might have been times you found other developers more experienced than you are, and not wanting to review their code. However, in such a situation, reviewing good or poor-quality code can improve you as a developer drastically. Along the way, I found myself searching and learning a lot about things I didn't know.
This resource was the most important piece that helped me to make an impactful review.

Better open-source player 👾

After accomplishing all the goals I counted above, ironically it feels easier to do them again. Before I attempted any of those, it was very overwhelming, they all seemed like it was out of my capability. Do not wait for your work to be perfect and get started with what you truly love.
The feeling of accomplishment is so rewarding that you will be able to say; "you could do it once again."
item.author: batunpc

item.title: 📢 Spring Boot 3.0 正式发布 🚀 | 📢 Spring Boot 3.0 Goes GA 🚀
item.description:
从今天开始，期待已久的Spring Boot 3今天发布了：

下一代 Spring Boot 来了！ 非常感谢所有为此版本提供帮助的人。

根据发布说明：

此版本是 12 个月的工作和 151 个不同个人的5700 多次提交的结晶。 非常感谢所有做出贡献的人，以及所有为里程碑提供重要反馈的早期采用者。

这是自 4.5 年前发布 2.0 以来，Spring Boot 的第一次重大修订。 它也是第一个支持Spring Framework 6.0和 GraalVM 的 Spring Boot GA 版本。

新版本的亮点包括：

功能太多，无法在此处详细列出，因此请前往我们 wiki 上的发行说明页面了解更多信息。

加入 DEV 和 MongoDB，使用 MongoDB Atlas 构建前端应用程序。 在DEV x MongoDB Atlas Hackathon 2022中，更改流以在数据库更改时显示实时更新。

The next generation of Spring Boot is here! Massive thank you to everyone who's helped with this release.

spring.io/blog/2022/11/2…

According to the releaseNote :

This release is the culmination of 12 months work and over 5700 commits by 151 different individuals. A massive thank you to everyone that has contributed, and to all the early adopters that have been providing vital feedback on the milestones.

This is the first major revision of Spring Boot since 2.0 was released 4.5 years ago. It’s also the first GA version of Spring Boot that provides support for Spring Framework 6.0 and GraalVM.

    原文:

    Sung-Chul Lee (이성철)

    Posted on Nov 25

    https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/35941
    유료 회원의 원성이 여기까지 느껴집니다

    한 CEO가 80M$를 빌려서 비트코인 채굴 회사에 이사회의 보고도 없이 넣어버렸습니다

    https://twitter.com/thogge/status/1595841911465517057

    브라질이 애플에 충전 규격을 맞추라는 압박을 리테일 스토어의 아이폰을 압수하는 방법으로 넣고 있습니다

    https://9to5mac.com/2022/11/24/brazil-seizes-iphones-retail-stores-charger-requirement/

    BBC 다큐멘터리 제작자들이 시위자의 정체를 숨기기 위해 얼굴 바꾸는 AI를 사용했습니다

    https://www.newscientist.com/article/2348197-bbc-documentary-used-face-swapping-ai-to-hide-protesters-identities/?utm_campaign=RSS%7CNSNS

    아치리눅스가 이제 20살입니다

    https://www.theregister.com/2022/03/15/arch_linux_20/

    상용 퍼시스턴스 API에 Rsync와 같은 기능을 하는 RClone이 이제 10살입니다

    https://forum.rclone.org/t/rclone-is-10-years-old-today/34185

    tRPC를 graphql 대용으로 사용하는 회사의 사례입니다

    https://echobind.com/post/why-we-ditched-graphql-for-trpc
    핵심 이유는

    • 보일러 플레이트의 적은 사용 (본래 보일러가 작은데다가, GraphQL의 중복 선언 문제가 없습니다)
    • 코드젠을 피할 수 있습니다
    • 클라이언트 번들 사이즈가 작습니다 (81kb -> 23.7)
    • 아폴로클라이언트의 캐시문제를 다루지 않아도 됩니다
    • IDE에서 환상적인 타입세이프 경험을 할 수 있습니다

    물론 RPC이므로

    • 필드별 요청을 잃습니다
    • 쿼리로서 인트로스펙션이 없습니다 (그런데 이미 인터페이스가 확고하게 타입세이프니 큰 문제가 아닐 듯합니다)
    • 타입스크립트만 백엔드와 서버에서 사용해야 합니다

    러스트는 멋지지만 스타트업에서 사용할 때 조심할 점을 정리한 글입니다

    https://mdwdotla.medium.com/using-rust-at-a-startup-a-cautionary-tale-42ab823d9454 이분은 2년 넘게 러스트를 사용하여 CRUD + REST + gRPC 끝점을 개발하셨습니다.

    심지어 그회사에서는 Rust 시니어 개발자가 있었습니다.

    그런데 코드 사이즈가 커질수록 러스트를 사용하는 세금이 커진다고 느꼈다고 합니다.

    • 러스트는 러닝커브가 큰편입니다 (개발자 수가 10배로 커질 때 발생한 이슈)
    • 개발이 느리게 진행되며 생산성이 더디다a
    • Rust 개발자 고용이 어렵다
    • 라이브러리와 그 문서가 아직 성숙되지 않았다고 합니다

    Emacs를 iPad에 올리려는 시도가 담겨있는 블로그입니다

    https://andschwa.com/posts/emacs-on-an-ipad/ Emacs는 좋은 에디터 중 하나입니다.

    얼마나 좋으면 어떻게든 Emacs를 올려보려고 할까요?

    Philip M. Gollucci - Mar 27

item.title: 2022 年十大远程工作工具和软件 | 2022's Top 10 Remote Work Tools and Software
item.description:
近年来，远程工作的人气激增，预计这一趋势将持续下去。 远程工作人员需要一套正确的工具和软件才能在任何地点高效地工作。 随着我们进入 2022 年，有几种顶级的远程工作工具和软件产品在竞争中脱颖而出。

此外，Microsoft Teams 还集成了其他流行的应用程序，例如 Office365、SharePoint Online 和 OneDrive。 这种强大的组合使其成为希望在远离办公室的同时保持联系和高效的远程团队的理想解决方案。

它还具有线程对话、直接消息和渠道等功能，可帮助团队保持井井有条并专注于手头的任务。

凭借其简化的界面和用户友好的功能（例如屏幕共享和虚拟白板），它使所有参与者都可以轻松地从任何位置进行实时协作。 此外，Google Hangouts Meet 还集成了其他流行的应用程序，例如 Gmail、Google 日历和 Google Drive，以提供更多便利。

它提供基于云的电话、呼叫路由和移动应用程序集成等功能，以帮助团队随时随地保持联系。 此外，Vonage Business Cloud 允许轻松扩展，因此您永远不必担心随着团队的扩大而用完电话线或分机号。

凭借其拖放式界面和可定制的看板，远程工作人员可以轻松地保持井井有条并专注于及时完成任务。 此外，Trello 还集成了其他流行的应用程序，例如 Gmail 和 Dropbox，以增加便利性。

其多功能平台允许用户分配任务、跟踪进度并与团队成员实时协作。 此外，Asana 还集成了其他流行的应用程序，例如 Slack 和 Google Drive，以增加协作和便利性。

最后，Zoom 是虚拟会议的首选。 其用户友好的界面让您只需点击几下即可轻松设置通话、共享屏幕以及录制音频或视频会话。 此外，Zoom 还提供分组讨论室和白板功能等功能，使其成为希望在远离办公室时保持联系的远程团队的理想解决方案。

这些只是 2022 年可用的一些顶级远程工作工具和软件，无论他们在哪里工作，它们都可以帮助保持团队的生产力和联系。 通过将基于云的工具、视频会议、文件共享和任务管理功能全部集中在一处，这些应用程序使您可以轻松地保持井井有条并从任何位置完成工作。



原文:

Remote working has seen a huge surge in popularity in recent years and this trend is expected to continue. Remote workers require the right set of tools and software to enable efficient work from any location. As we move into 2022, there are several top-rated remote work tools and software offerings that stand out among the competition.

Microsoft Teams has become an industry leader for remote collaboration, offering team messaging, video conferencing, file sharing, and task management capabilities all in one place. Additionally, Microsoft Teams integrates with other popular applications such as Office365, SharePoint Online, and OneDrive. This powerful combination makes it an ideal solution for remote teams looking to stay connected and productive while staying away from the office.

Slack is another reliable tool for communication and collaboration, offering messaging, file sharing, and task management capabilities. Slack is highly customizable with a wide range of integrations available to suit any team’s needs. It also has features like threaded conversations, direct messages, and channels that help teams stay organized and focused on the task at hand.

Google Hangouts Meet is another great application for remote meetings. With its streamlined interface and user-friendly features such as screen sharing and virtual whiteboards, it makes it easy for all participants to collaborate in real-time from any location. Additionally, Google Hangouts Meet integrates with other popular applications such as Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Drive for added convenience.

Vonage Business Cloud is an effective phone system for remote teams. It provides features like cloud-based telephony, call routing, and mobile app integration to help keep teams connected on the go. Additionally, Vonage Business Cloud allows for easy scalability so that you never have to worry about running out of phone lines or extensions as your team grows.

Trello is a popular project management software that helps users organize their tasks in a visual way. With its drag-and-drop interface and customizable boards, it makes it easy for remote workers to stay organized and focused on completing their tasks in a timely manner. Additionally, Trello integrates with other popular applications such as Gmail and Dropbox for added convenience.

Asana is another great tool for managing projects remotely. Its versatile platform lets users assign tasks, track progress, and collaborate with team members in real time. Additionally, Asana integrates with other popular applications such as Slack and Google Drive for added collaboration and convenience.

Finally, Zoom is the go-to choice for virtual meetings. Its user-friendly interface makes it easy to set up calls, share screens, and record audio or video sessions with just a few clicks. Additionally, Zoom offers features like breakout rooms and whiteboard functionality that make it an ideal solution for remote teams looking to stay connected while staying away from the office.

These are just some of the top remote work tools and software available for 2022 that can help keep teams productive and connected no matter where their work takes them. With cloud-based tools, video conferencing, file sharing, and task management capabilities all in one place, these applications make it easy to stay organized and get things done from any location.

item.title: 为什么应避免环境变量的默认值 | Why default values for environment variables should be avoided
item.description:
假设您期望某个特定变量存在，如果它不存在，您应该怎么办？ 我说立即停止应用程序。

根据我的经验，默认值是开发中几乎所有罪恶的根源。 如果您提供默认值，则意味着您不关心提供的值，因为您可以随时将其替换为其他值。

这意味着您不关心提供的值，因为您可以随时用其他东西替换它。

始终将配置与代码分开也是一个好习惯。

默认值会使调试错误配置的应用程序变得更加困难，因为最终配置值可能是硬编码默认值和环境变量的组合。

完全依赖环境变量（或尽可能多地依赖）意味着您拥有关于应用程序配置方式的单一事实来源，从而使故障排除更加容易。

如果您喜欢这个故事，我们的博客会更喜欢它。 查看dev.to/clickpesa 、 medium.com/clickpesa-engineering-blog或clickpesa.hashnode.dev

作为 JavaScript 开发者，我们通常不必自己处理编译器。 然而，了解 JavaScript 引擎的基础知识并了解它如何处理我们对人类友好的 JS 代码并将其转化为机器可以理解的代码绝对是一件好事！ 编码愉快！

解除暂停后，clickpesa 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，clickpesa 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 clickpesa 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

报告其他不当行为

原文:

Assuming you expect a particular variable to be present, what should you do if it is not present? I say stop the application immediately.

Based on my experience, default values are the root of almost all evil in development. If you provide default values, means you do not care about the supplied value because you can at any time substitute it with something else.

It means you do not care about the supplied value because you can at any time substitute it with something else.

It is also a good practice to always split configuration from code.

Default values can make debugging a misconfigured application more difficult, as the final config values will likely be a combination of hard-coded default values and environment variables.

Relying purely on environment variables (or as much as possible) means you have a single source of truth for how your application was configured, making troubleshooting easier.

If you enjoyed this story, our blogs have more like it. Checkout dev.to/clickpesa, medium.com/clickpesa-engineering-blog or clickpesa.hashnode.dev

Classic DEV Post from 2020:

🚀⚙️ JavaScript Visualized: the JavaScript Engine

As JavaScript devs, we usually don't have to deal with compilers ourselves. However, it's definitely good to know the basics of the JavaScript engine and see how it handles our human-friendly JS code, and turns it into something machines understand! 🥳

Happy coding!

item.title: Reverse A Positive Integer：可视化代码教程 | Reverse A Positive Integer: a visual code tutorial
item.description:
有一些新工具可以生成很酷的短代码片段演示，其中之一是wave snippets 。

我整理了一个非常基本问题的小演示：编写一个函数来反转正整数。

意思是取一个像123这样的数字，让它变成321 。 知道了？

我是用打字稿写的，并没有处理很多额外的东西，但它适用于简单的情况。 一探究竟：

这是完成的代码：

 const reversePositiveInt = ( toReverse : number ): number => { let source = toReverse let accumulator = 0 while ( true ) { if ( source <= 0 ) { break } const mod = source % 10 accumulator += mod source = Math . floor ( source / 10 ) accumulator *= 10 } return accumulator / 10 } console . log ( reversePositiveInt ( 12345 )) // 54321

这样做的方法是将输入数字的最后一位剃掉，并将其放在一个新整数的前面，一次一个数字。

有一种方法可以更快地做到这一点，但我认为这样一步一步地写，可以更容易地看到发生了什么。

前往我们的欢迎帖，向我们介绍一下您自己！

古斯塔沃·吉夏尔—— 一旦暂停，ard 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

一旦解除暂停，ard 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging ard will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning ard 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

There are some new tools to generate cool demos of short code snippets, one of these is wave snippets.

I put together a small demo of a very basic problem: write a function to reverse a positive integer.

Meaning take a number like 123 and make it 321. Got it?

I wrote this in typescript, and didn't handle a lot of extra stuff but it works for simple cases. Check it out:

Here is the finished code:

const reversePositiveInt = (toReverse: number):number => { let source = toReverse let accumulator = 0 while(true) { if(source <= 0) { break } const mod = source % 10 accumulator += mod source = Math.floor(source / 10) accumulator *= 10 } return accumulator / 10
} console.log(reversePositiveInt(12345))
// 54321

The method of doing is this is to shave off the last digit of the input number and put it at the front of a new integer one number at a time.

There is a way to do it faster, but written like this step by step I think it makes it easier to see what is going on.

Head over to our Welcome Thread and tell us a bit about yourself!

Gustavo Guichard - Nov 14

item.title: 在取得进展的同时确定优先次序 | Prioritization while making progress
item.description:
乍得·R·斯图尔特

发表于我想快速跟进 making progress 文章，因为我最近意识到我的方法中缺少一些东西。

所以我提到了如何制定可以帮助你实现目标的任务，但我没有说如何确定它们的优先级。 实际上，您提出的所有任务都具有相同的权重，并且预计您每周完成尽可能多的任务。 这没有考虑到它们的重要性或它们推动您实现目标的可能性。 所以我想快速谈谈这个作为补充信息。

什么是优先级

因此，确定优先级是确定您可以利用现有时间做的最有影响力的事情的行为。 例如，您有一份作为软件开发人员的任务列表。 您可以修复可能导致中断的错误，或者您可以开发新功能。 您确信该功能可以为您的软件添加新用户，但您已经确定出现错误的可能性非常高。 因此，您决定立即着手修复该错误。 情况可能不像这个例子那么直截了当，但这让你知道这会是什么样子。

如何确定优先级

那么，您如何确定任务的优先级？ 老实说，我自己对做这件事还很陌生，所以我主要要听取Kun The Engineer的意见，他制作了一个关于这个主题的视频：

作为软件工程师如何进行优先级排序（来自 Microsoft 的 L8 SWE）

在这段视频中，Kun 谈到了作为工程负责人确定优先级的重要性，然后给出了如何确定任务优先级的流程。 为了简要介绍视频，他建议使用一个系统，他提到该系统已经过多个团队的实战测试，并被证明非常有效。 该系统是优先级 = 影响 x 置信度 / 成本。 此处的影响是活动成功的价值，信心是您对完成任务的信心，成本是尝试完成任务所需的估计时间（此示例以天为单位）。 我不想从视频中拿走太多，我强烈建议观看它，因为他通过示例很好地解释了系统。

他还有一个工作表，他在他的博客中分享了关于该主题的工作表，您可以在此处找到该工作表。

我之所以要指出这一点，是因为我意识到这个过程与我在上一篇文章中计划你的一周时谈到的习惯非常相得益彰。 虽然他研究的示例更长期，但它可用于更短期的目标。 这里的主要优势是添加数据。 就我个人而言，我每周都会列一个清单，说明我每周需要做什么，我会添加我所做的工作来确定我的优先级。 虽然数据远非精确，但它会让我了解我正在承担的任务类型的影响，这会让我知道是否需要将注意力转移到其他事情上。

结论

所以我只是想快速谈谈这个，因为我认为这将是对我之前谈到的内容的一个很好的补充。 我仍在学习自己，但这绝对是一项需要指出的重要技能，所以我也想与大家分享！

  • 如果您觉得本文有趣，请随时为本文点赞！
  • 如果您有兴趣了解有关前端工程的更多信息，请在Dev.to和Twitter上关注我。
  • 如果您正在找工作，我强烈建议您在Twitter 、 LinkedIn或 TechIsHiring 的新网站https://www.TechIsHiring.com/上查看@TechIsHiring，以获取已发布的职位和其他资源！

解除暂停后，chad_r_stewart 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

取消发布后，chad_r_stewart 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 chad_r_stewart 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

原文:

Chad R. Stewart

Posted on Nov 25

I wanted to quickly follow up on the making progress article because I realized recently that there was something missing from my approach.

So I mentioned how to make tasks that will help you reach your goals but I didn’t say how to prioritize them. Effectively, all the tasks you come up with would have equal weight and it would be expected that you complete as many of them as you can each week. This doesn’t take into account their importance or their likeliness to move you towards your goals. So I wanted to talk about this quickly as supplemental information.

What is Prioritization

So prioritization is the act of identifying what are the most impactful things you could do with the time you have. For example, you have a list of tasks as a Software Developer. You could fix a bug that could potentially cause an outage or you could work on a new feature. You are confident the feature can add new users to your software but you’ve identified that the likelihood of the bug to occur is pretty high. So you decide to immediately work on fixing the bug. Situations may not be as straight-forward as this example but this gives you an idea of what this would look like.

How to Prioritize

So how do you prioritize your tasks? Honestly, I’m pretty new to doing this myself so I’m primarily going to defer to Kun The Engineer who created a video on this topic:

How to do Prioritization as a Software Engineer (From L8 SWE at Microsoft)

In this video, Kun talks about the importance of prioritization as an Engineering Leader and then gives a process on how to prioritize tasks. To give a quick synopsis of the video, he proposes using a system which he mentioned has been battle-tested by several teams and has been shown to be very effective. The system is Priority = Impact x Confidence / Cost. Impact here being the value of the activity being successful, Confidence is how confident you are in being able to accomplish the task and Cost being the estimated amount of time (this example is in days) it would take to attempt to complete the task. I don’t want to take too much away from the video, I highly recommend watching it as he does a great job of explaining the system by going through an example.

He also has a worksheet that he shares in his blog on this topic which you can find here.

The reason why I wanted to point this out is I realized that this process works very nicely with the habits I talk about in planning for your week in the previous article. While the example he works on is more long-term, it can be used for more short-term goals. The main strength here is the addition of data. Personally I make a list every week to say what I need to do each week and I’ll be adding the work I did to come up with my prioritization. While the data is far from precise, it’ll give me an idea of the types of tasks I’m taking on in terms of impact and this’ll give me an idea if I need to switch my focus to something else.

Conclusion

So I just wanted to touch on this quickly cause I think this would be a great addition to what I had talked about earlier. I am still learning myself but this is definitely an important skill to point out and so I wanted to share this with everyone as well!

  • If you found this article interesting, please feel free to heart this article!
  • If you’re interested in learning more about Front-End Engineering, follow me here on Dev.to and Twitter.
  • If you’re looking for jobs, I’d highly recommend checking out @TechIsHiring on Twitter, LinkedIn or TechIsHiring's new website https://www.TechIsHiring.com/ for posted jobs and other resources!

item.title: 内容结构：内容创建的注意事项 | Content Structure: The Do's and Dont's of Content Creation
item.description:
关于如何制作精彩内容的许多提示和技巧。

背景

我的名字是 Maxi Contieri。 我来自阿根廷。

我在软件行业从事教学和工作近 30 年。

在 covid-19 大流行之前，我写了 0 篇文章。

现在，我已经写了 300 并且还在数着……

这里有一些写博客的技巧。

这些是我在旅途中学到的建议。

做的

找到你热衷的事情。

理想情况下，您应该稍微走出自己的舒适区。

做你的研究并学习新事物。

你可以做一些搜索引擎优化研究来最大限度地提高参与度，但我鼓励你写下你的激情。

寻找社区

在社交网络、论坛、博客、Discord 等上关注合适的人（需要时间）。

不要将个人社交网络与专业社交网络混在一起，因为这会导致 拖延。

我将Twitter 、 Linkedin 、 Discord和RSS 提要用于专业用途。

我有 TikTok、Instagram 和其他 RSS 提要供个人/娱乐使用。

创建文章模板

使用它们作为您的签名。

从一个空的结构开始比从一张空白的画布开始要好得多。

这是我的代码味道系列中的一个示例。

使用正确的工具

大多数作家使用这些工具（特别是如果英语不是你的母语，我的不是）。

顺便说一句，本文照片由Ryan Snaadt在Unsplash上拍摄

创建您自己的工具

  • 您可以开发自己的解析或验证工具或使用开源工具。

交叉发布和元数据

使用正确的 SEO 和 Canonical 工具。

向您的博客添加一些分析（如果可用）并了解人们如何找到您的文章。

使用系列

读者喜欢看到带有交叉引用的相互交织的短篇文章，以了解每篇文章如何与其他文章相辅相成。

添加一个 TL；DR：

使用情感标题和图像

我们阅读了所有文章的标题和图片，然后决定要阅读的内容。

使用情绪化和吸引人的标题。

不要撒谎或使用点击诱饵标题。 人类不喜欢骗子和骗子。

这篇文章的第一个标题是：

一些制作好文章的技巧。

现在它是：

关于如何制作精彩内容的许多提示和技巧。

与案例惯例保持一致

选择一些惯例并坚持下去。

我更喜欢Title Case而不是 Sentence case。

养成习惯

写作是一种习惯。

阅读如何掌握它们并破解你的大脑。

足够好时发布。 不迟

不要等到文章完美。

做一些校对。 等几天再读一遍。

更正上面的词:)。

园艺

让您的文章保持活力和新鲜感。

准备好用其他人的建设性反馈和想法来更新它们。

经常去拜访他们。

使用（代码）示例

人们通过实例而不是理论思想来学习。

无论理论、基础知识和证据多么有趣，读者都会直接看例子。

生产力黑客

遵循一些提高效率的技巧以保持专注。

您可以设置自己的微奖励、婴儿学步目标、禁用所有通知等等。

跟踪您的代码示例

为您的代码示例使用 GIT 跟踪器并将其粘贴到您的文章中。

不要使用 iframe，因为它们速度较慢并且可能无法在所有设备上加载

您可以将您的代码使用降价注释来跟踪隐藏的外部引用。

在移动设备上查看您的文章

您可能在个人计算机上编写和测试您的文章。

你的读者不会。

请务必使用 AMP/PWA 页面和窄代码块。

不要做

不要保留进行中的文章。

享受势头。

乘风破浪！

打开太多标签

不要保留太多想法或太多标签。

解决它们并关闭它们。

造型

不要滥用样式。

人们会厌倦试图找到他们的意义。

避免被动语态

被动句比主动句读得慢。

你读主动句子的速度比被动句子快。

不要边写边说

我们使用不同的结构和语法进行交谈和写作。

你需要写很短的句子。

对抗冒名顶替综合症

准备好对抗冒名顶替综合症。

你不是最适合写作的人，也永远不会是，没有人天生就是专家。

对抗完美主义

一篇不完美的发表文章胜过 10.000 个想法

不要喂巨魔

人们有意见。

回答并与好人讨论。

你可以从他们那里学到新的观点。

不要喂巨魔。 使用可用的审核工具。

练习，练习，练习

如果你盖了，他们会来的。



原文:

Many tips and tricks on how to make amazing content.

TL;DR: Follow these experience tips to improve your technical articles.

Background

My name is Maxi Contieri. I am From Argentina.

I've been teaching and working in the software industry for almost 30 years.

I had written 0 Articles before the covid-19 pandemic.

Now, I've written 300 and counting...

Here are a couple of tips for blogging.

These are the pieces of advice I learned on my journey.

Do's

Find something you are passionate about.

Ideally, you should be a bit out of your comfort zone.

Do your research and learn new things.

You can do some SEO research to maximize engagement, but I encourage you to write about your passions.

Find a Community

Follow the right people (takes time) on social networks, forums, blogs, Discord, etc.

Don't mix personal social networks with professional ones since this favors procrastination.

I use Twitter, Linkedin, Discord, and RSS feeds for professional usage.

I have TikTok, Instagram, and other RSS feeds for personal/joy.

Create Article Templates

Use them as your signature.

Starting from an empty structure is much better than working from a blank canvas.

Here's an example from my code smell series.

Use the Right Tools

Most writers use tools like these (especially if English is not your mother language, mine isn't).

By the way, this article photo by Ryan Snaadt on Unsplash

Create Your Own Tools

  • You can develop your own parsing or validating tools or use open-source ones.

Crossposting and Metadata

Use the right SEO and Canonical Tools.

Add some analytics to your blog (if available) and understand how people find your articles.

Use Series

Readers love to see short intertwined articles with cross-references to see how each article complements others.

Add a TL;DR:

TL;DRs are a nice way to tell your reader you value his/her time.

Use Emotive Titles and Images

We read all articles' titles and images and decide which we will read.

Use emotional and catchy headlines.

Don't lie or use click bait titles. Humans don't like cheaters and liars.

This article's first heading was:

Some tips to make good articles.

Now it is:

Many tips and tricks on how to make amazing content.

Be Consistent on Case Conventions

Choose some convention and stick with it.

I prefer Title Case over Sentence case.

Create Habits

Writing is a habit.

Read how to master them and hack your brain.

Publish When it is Good Enough. Not Later

Don't wait until the article is perfect.

Do some proofreeding. Wait a couple of days and read it again.

Correct the above word :).

Gardening

Keep your articles alive and fresh.

Be ready to update them with constructive feedback and ideas from other people.

Go revisit them often.

Use (code) Samples

People learn by examples, not by theoretical ideas.

No matter how interesting the theory, fundamentals, and evidence are, readers will go straight to the examples.

Productivity Hacks

Follow some productivity tips to stay focused.

You can set your own micro rewards, baby steps goals, disable all notifications, and many others.

Track Your Code Samples

Use a GIT tracker for your code samples and paste it on your articles.

Don't use iframes since they are slower and might not load on every device

You can put your code using markdown comments to track hidden external references.

Check Your Articles on Mobile Devices

You probably write and test your articles on a personal computer.

Your readers won't.

Be sure to use AMP/PWA pages and narrow code blocks.

Don'ts

Don't keep work-in-progress articles.

Enjoy the momentum.

Ride the wave!

Open Too Many Tabs

Don't keep too many ideas or too many tabs.

Address them and close them.

Styling

Don't abuse styling.

People get tired trying to find their meaning.

Avoid Passive Voice

Passive sentences are read slower than active ones.

You read active sentences faster than passive ones.

Don't Write as You Speak

We talk and write using different structures and grammar.

You need to write very short sentences.

HemmingwayApp does the magic for you.

Fight the Impostor's Syndrome

Be ready to fight impostor syndrome.

You are not the most suitable person to write nor will you ever be and no one is born an expert.

Fight Perfectionism

One not-perfect published article is better than 10.000 ideas

Don't Feed the Troll

People have opinions.

Answer and discuss with nice people.

You can learn new points of view from them.

Don't feed the trolls. Use available moderation tools.

Practice, Practice, Practice

If you build it, they will come.
item.author: mcsee

item.title: 最后，你问的是 Stack Overflow 吗？ | Afinal, o que é Stack Overflow?
item.description:
你问过 StackOverflow 上的任何问题，你知道什么意义吗？ 调用栈

堆栈溢出

Podemos resumir ele como sendo um problema causado por chamar várias e várias funções uma dentro da outra, aumentando em muito o tamanho da nossa Call Stack, até o momento em que o nosso código acaba por se quebrar. Podemos resumir ele como sendo um problema causado por chamar várias e várias funções uma dentro da outra, aumentando em muito o tamanho da nossa Call Stack, até o momento em que o nosso código acaba por se quebrar。

Acredite ou não, isso é surpreendentemente fácil de se fazer com uma recursão não muito bem pensada ou coisas parecidas, como exemplicado a seguir: Accredite ou não, isso é surpreendentemente fácil de se fazer com uma recursão não muito bem pensada ou coisas parecidas, como examplicado a seguir:

 function inception (){ inception () } incepetion ()

Como da pra imaginar, essa função vai ficar se chamando infinitamente, aumentando o tamanho da Call Stack até essa acabar quebrando e nos retornando um Uncaught RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded . Como da pra imaginar, essa função vai ficar se chamando infinitamente, aumentando o tamanho da Call Stack até essa acabar quebrando e nos retornando um Uncaught RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded 。

Uma coisa interessante é que antigamente os navegadores não tinham esse erro, eles simplesmente rodavam o código infinitamente até eventualmente crashar completamente, muitas vezes até mesmo travando o computador do usuário. Uma coisa interessante é que antigamente os navegadores não tinham esse erro, eles simplesmente rodavam o código infinitamente até eventualmente crashar completamente, muitas vezes até mesmo travando o computador do usuário. Esse erro surgiu para evitar esse tipo de problema. Esse erro surgiu para evitar esse tipo de problema。

Resumindo, o Stack Overflow nada mais é do que colocar carga demais em uma Call Stack, fazendo com que a mesma acabe quebrando. Resumindo, o Stack Overflow nada mais é do que colocar carga demais em uma Call Stack, fazendo com que a mesma acabe quebrando。

E com essa leitura você acabou de adquirir um conhecimento para puxar aleatoriamente em uma call com seus colegas enquanto espera o ambiente de desenvolvimento carregar ou quando fica um silêncio na hora do build. E com essa leitura você acabou de adquirir um conhecimento para puxar aleatoriamente em uma call com seus colegas enquanto espera o ambiente de desenvolvimento carregar ou quando fica um silêncio na hora do build。



原文:

Se você ta lendo isso aqui, eu tenho 99,9% de certeza que em algum momento o StackOverflow acabou salvando sua manhã, tarde ou madrugada e tudo que eu posso dizer pra você sobre isso é: Seja muito bem vindo ao clube.

Mas você já se questionou de onde veio o nome StackOverflow ou o que ele significa? Eu já, nos primórdios dos meus estudos com programação aonde eu gastava mais tempo lendo e relendo tópicos nesta plataforma do que realmente escrevendo código.

Esse questionamento me voltou a mente em uma conversa com alguns amigos que utilizam o site todo santo dia, sendo até mesmo muito ativos dentro da comunidade mas que não fazem ideia do que é um Stack Overflow.

Call Stack

O Call Stack tem um trabalho simples e extremamente importante, orientando em aonde estamos no código para que possamos rodar aquelas instruções na ordem correta para que tudo funcione como o planejado.

De uma outra forma, pense nele como literalmente uma pilha com a ordem de chamada das funções do seu código. Um espaço na memória que vai armazenar as funções que você chama no seu código, adicionando um novo item ao topo da pilha sempre que você chama uma nova função e removendo este ao término da sua execução.

E agora, o questionamento que talvez você esteja se fazendo é o que acontece no caso de uma função chamar outra. Isso é bem simples se seguirmos o que já foi explicado, a função que foi chamada vai novamente para o topo da pilha e passa a ser executada, sendo retirada e dando lugar a próxima assim que terminar e assim sucessivamente. Acho que já deu pra entender né?

Stack Overflow

Podemos resumir ele como sendo um problema causado por chamar várias e várias funções uma dentro da outra, aumentando em muito o tamanho da nossa Call Stack, até o momento em que o nosso código acaba por se quebrar.

Acredite ou não, isso é surpreendentemente fácil de se fazer com uma recursão não muito bem pensada ou coisas parecidas, como exemplicado a seguir:

function inception(){ inception()
} incepetion()

Como da pra imaginar, essa função vai ficar se chamando infinitamente, aumentando o tamanho da Call Stack até essa acabar quebrando e nos retornando um Uncaught RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded.

Uma coisa interessante é que antigamente os navegadores não tinham esse erro, eles simplesmente rodavam o código infinitamente até eventualmente crashar completamente, muitas vezes até mesmo travando o computador do usuário. Esse erro surgiu para evitar esse tipo de problema.

Resumindo, o Stack Overflow nada mais é do que colocar carga demais em uma Call Stack, fazendo com que a mesma acabe quebrando.

E com essa leitura você acabou de adquirir um conhecimento para puxar aleatoriamente em uma call com seus colegas enquanto espera o ambiente de desenvolvimento carregar ou quando fica um silêncio na hora do build.
item.author: hiago75

item.title: Yet Another Horizo​​ntal Scroller（在反应中） | Yet Another Horizontall Scroller ( in react)
item.description:
布拉姆锤

发表于一个简单而轻量级的组件，允许您在移动设备和桌面设备上水平滚动项目列表，并具有小的“拉伸”效果。

我找不到足够快的东西，所以我自己做了一个，它简单有效。 您遇到任何问题请告诉我们！

安装：

 npm install yah-scroller --save-dev

或者

用法 ：

将YahScroller添加到您的组件：

 import React from ' react ' import ReactDOM from ' react-dom/client ' import { YahScroller } from ' yah-scroller ' import ' yah-scroller/assets/style.scss ' ; // you can include the css version if needed const root = ReactDOM . createRoot ( document . getElementById ( ' root ' ) as HTMLElement ) root . render ( < React . StrictMode > < div > < h2 > Simple scroller < /h2 > < YahScroller className = " horizontal-scroll " > < div key = { ' c1 ' } className = { ' category ' } > Category 1 < /div > < div key = { ' c2 ' } className = { ' category ' } > Category 2 < /div > < div key = { ' c3 ' } className = { ' category ' } > Category 3 < /div > < div key = { ' c4 ' } className = { ' category ' } > Category 4 < /div > < div key = { ' c5 ' } className = { ' category ' } > Category 5 < /div > < div key = { ' c6 ' } className = { ' category ' } > Category 6 < /div > < /YahScroller > < /div > < /React.StrictMode> , )

你喜欢它？ 或者想要添加什么？

请告诉我们！
我打算添加的内容：

  • 使用某人希望使用它的样式化组件的不同版本
  • 更多的定制可能
  • 在垂直或水平之间切换
  • 示例页面

将您最喜欢的 Google Cloud API 与 MongoDB Atlas 结合使用，为您参加DEV x MongoDB Atlas 黑客马拉松 2022构建高度可扩展的现代应用程序。

原文:

Bram Hammer

Posted on Nov 24

Yet another horizontal scroller for React.
A simple and lightweight component that allows you to scroll horizontally through a list of items on both mobile and desktop with a small 'stretch' effect.

I couldn't find something fast enough so I made one myself it's simple and effective. Do you encounter any issues please let us know!

Installation: 

npm install yah-scroller --save-dev

or

Usage :

Add YahScroller to your component:

import React from 'react'
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client'
import { YahScroller } from 'yah-scroller'
import 'yah-scroller/assets/style.scss'; // you can include the css version if needed const root = ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root') as HTMLElement)
root.render( <React.StrictMode> <div> <h2>Simple scroller</h2>
 <YahScroller className="horizontal-scroll"> <div key={'c1'} className={'category'}>Category 1</div>
 <div key={'c2'} className={'category'}>Category 2</div>
 <div key={'c3'} className={'category'}>Category 3</div>
 <div key={'c4'} className={'category'}>Category 4</div>
 <div key={'c5'} className={'category'}>Category 5</div>
 <div key={'c6'} className={'category'}>Category 6</div>
 </YahScroller>
 </div>
 </React.StrictMode>,
)

You like it? Or want something to be added?

Please let us know!
What I plan to add:

  • different version to use styled components of someone whishes to use it
  • more customisations possible
  • switch between vertical or horizontal
  • example page(s)

Use your favorite Google Cloud APIs with MongoDB Atlas to build a highly-scalable modern application for your entry in the DEV x MongoDB Atlas Hackathon 2022.

→ Get Started

item.title: 过渡网络 w/ Chris Ferdinandi | The Transitional Web w/ Chris Ferdinandi
item.description:
什么是 HTML 万物

该播客涉及网络开发主题以及经营小型企业、个体经营和时间管理。 您可以加入他们的行列，了解他们的成功和他们的奋斗，因为他们试图管理扩展他们的 Web 开发业务，而又不会让自己过于紧张。

这是关于什么的？

本周我们与 Chris Ferdinandi 坐下来讨论为什么他认为网络处于过渡状态，从臃肿的框架和其他工具转向使用更多“香草”或“本机”平台功能。 显示注释

  • 在您的博文中，您提到“网络正处于过渡状态”，您可以对此进行扩展吗？ 什么是精益网络与“臃肿网络”？ 像 React 和 Vue 这样的大型框架仍在行业中大量使用，您认为为什么会发生变化？ 我们将使用什么来代替？ 您认为这些“过渡工具”会永远存在吗？ 在您看来，理想的“网络平台”是什么样的？ 链接

原文:

What is HTML All The Things

HTML All The Things is a web development podcast and discord community which was started by Matt and Mike, developers based in Ontario, Canada.

The podcast speaks to web development topics as well as running a small business, self-employment and time management. You can join them for both their successes and their struggles as they try to manage expanding their Web Development business without stretching themselves too thin.

What's This One About?

This week we sat down with Chris Ferdinandi to discuss why he thinks the web is in a state of transition, moving away from bloated frameworks and other tooling, towards the use of more "vanilla" or "native" platform features. This less bloated web is where Chris believes we are already transitioning towards, with the hopes that it makes websites and web apps more efficient.

Show Notes

  • In your blog post you mention “the web is in a state of transition”, can you expand on that? - 00:03:30
  • What is the lean web vs “the Bloated Web”? - 00:13:59
  • Large frameworks like React and Vue are still being heavily used in the industry, why do you think that is set to change? What will we be using instead? - 00:34:00
  • Do you think these ‘transitional tools’ will be around forever? - 00:41:56
  • What would the ideal ‘web platform’ look like to you? - 00:50:50

Links

Thank you!

item.title: 先科自动修复 | SAST Autofix
item.description:
阿图尔香脂

发表于我相信自动化。 至于 2022 年，这不是很有启发性，也不是大胆的声明。

但是来自安全领域的大多数工具不包括任何类型的自动化，或者更糟的是，将其做不必要的复杂化。 尤其是修复静态应用程序安全测试工具 (SAST) 的结果，应该不会太麻烦。 毕竟，它是在寻找模式或代码中的精确匹配。

这让我开始思考：是否可以制定简单的修复规则。 我很高兴地说，我找到了！ 事不宜迟，所讨论的工具是： sed

主意

高层次的想法是：当找到代码的漏洞部分时，可以应用修复（如果可用），代码中不再存在漏洞。 所有这一切都用简单的 sed 命令。

如果你不记得 sed 是什么

流编辑器用于对输入流（文件或来自管道的输入）执行基本文本转换。

由于它好得令人难以置信 - 让我们检查一下sed的功能，以及我们如何潜在地使用它来对抗易受攻击的代码。

执行

带有s命令的sed被识别为替换，能够将匹配正则表达式的出现更改为其他内容。

第一： s进入http

从简单的test.sh文件开始：

 curl http://internal.eks-asdf.com/asdf curl https://internal.eks-asdf.com/asdf

让我们从简单的场景开始：我们希望强制所有脚本使用https而不是http与内部 EKS 地址进行通信。 为实现这一点，我们可以嵌入简单的作业，更改所有 bash 文件中的所有事件：

 sed 's+http://internal.eks-asdf.com+https://internal.eks-asdf.com/asdf+g' test.sh

正如我们可能争论的那样，如果此示例与从 http 迁移到 https（这是安全“事物”）更相关，那么事实是，它确实有效。

第二：SQLi

转到更多与安全相关的场景：使用来自 golang 推荐的推荐： https ://go.dev/doc/database/sql-injection 我们可以使用以下规则删除fmt.Sprintf并将所有%s更改为? 有以下部分代码： 

 rows, err := db.Query(fmt.Sprintf("SELECT * FROM user WHERE id = %s", id))

并使用我们的“自动修复”：

 sed 's+(fmt.Sprintf++g;s+%s+?+g;s+.$++g' test.go

命令组合：删除(fmt.Sprintf ，将%s与?交换并删除最后一个字符.$ （应该是最新的)字符）。

我们将以该代码结束，输出将是有效且有效的 go sql 查询：

 rows, err := db.Query("SELECT * FROM user WHERE id = ?", id)

这是比我想象的更平易近人的规则：它适用于具有多个参数的查询，它是不言自明的，并且让一切变得更好。

可用性

最后一个问题：是否有可能以用户友好的方式将其实施到安全工作中。 从一开始， sed包就可以使用名为Scripts的配置文件，该文件存储多个替代命令。

结局

命令本身并不是非常复杂，除非您试图实现一些不平凡的事情。 然而，它会引发问题：如果它不是微不足道的，为什么不手动修复它？ 自动修复功能应该适用于通用且易于修复的漏洞。 您始终可以使用一些复杂的软件（例如grep ）使它们更加专业化。

提出的“规则”是最优的吗？ 这取决于，可能不是，但可能值得尝试 - 它可能比在所有存储库的所有文件中手动更改它更优化。 或者根本不修复它。

将这篇文章视为概念证明。

这是深思熟虑的 SAST 解决方案，具有强大的“用户群”和惊人的功能，其中之一就是漏洞的自动修复。 它处于实验阶段，因此您可以自己尝试。

链接

全栈保险库 - 一旦暂停，schreddies 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，schreddies 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，schreddies 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 schreddies 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 schreddies 的方法：

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging schreddies will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning schreddies 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Artur Balsam

Posted on Nov 24

I believe in automation. That's not very revealing, neither bold, statement, as for 2022.

But most tooling from security world does not include any kind of automation, or, to make it worse, do it unnecessary complicated. Especially fixing findings from static application security testing tools (SAST), shouldn't be much of a hassle. After all, it is operating on finding the patterns, or exact matches in the code.

That got me thinking: is it possible to make simple fixing rules. And I am delighted to say, I've found one! Without further due, the tool in question is: sed

Idea

The high level idea is: when vulnerable part of the code is found, the fix, if available, can be applied, and the vulnerability no longer exist in the code. And all that with simple sed command.

If you do not recall what the sed is

sed is a stream editor. A stream editor is used to perform basic text transformations on an input stream (a file or input from a pipeline).

As it seems to be too good to be true - let's examine the functionality of the sed, and how we could potentially used it against vulnerable code.

Execution

The sed with s command, recognised as substitution, is able to change occurrences of matching regex into something else.

First: s into http

Starting with simple test.sh file:

curl http://internal.eks-asdf.com/asdf 
curl https://internal.eks-asdf.com/asdf

Let's start with simple scenario: we would love to enforce all scripts to communicate, using https, rather than http to internal EKS address. To achieve that, we could embedded simple job, that changes all occurrences, in all, bash files:

sed 's+http://internal.eks-asdf.com+https://internal.eks-asdf.com/asdf+g' test.sh

The sed command simply states, that http://internal.eks-asdf.com should be replaced with https://internal.eks-asdf.com/asdf, for all occurrences in file.

As we could argue if this example is more associated with migrating from http to https (which is security 'thing'), the fact is, it worked.

Second: SQLi

Going to more security related scenarios: using recommendation from the golang recommendation: https://go.dev/doc/database/sql-injection we can use following rule to remove fmt.Sprintf and change all %s into ? - change assembly of statement as string into actual query. Having following part of code:

rows, err := db.Query(fmt.Sprintf("SELECT * FROM user WHERE id = %s", id))

And using our 'autofix':

sed 's+(fmt.Sprintf++g;s+%s+?+g;s+.$++g' test.go

Command is combined: removing (fmt.Sprintf, exchanging %s with ? and removing last char .$ (which should be the latest ) character).

We will end with that code, the output will be, valid, and working go sql query:

rows, err := db.Query("SELECT * FROM user WHERE id = ?", id)

It is way more approachable rule than I could imagine: it works for query with multiple parameters, it's self-explanatory, and make everything better.

Usability

The last question: is it possible to implement it user friendly way, into security job. Starting from beginning, sed package can work with configuration file, called Scripts, that stores multiple substitute commands.

Outro

The commands itself are not terribly complex, unless you are trying to achieve something non-trivial. However, it will rise question: if it's not trivial, why not to fix it manually? The autofix feature should work for generic, and easy to fix vulnerabilities. You can always make them more specialized with some sophisticated software, like grep.

Are presented 'rules' optimal? It depends, probably not, but it might be worth to try - it might be more optimal than changing it manually in all files, of all repositories. Or not fixing it at all.

Treat this post as proof of concept.

Semgrep

It's well thought SAST solution, with great 'user base' and amazing features, and one of them is the automated fixing of the vulnerabilities. It is in the experimental phase, so you can try it yourself.

Links

https://www.nongnu.org/bibledit/sed_rules_reference.html
https://semgrep.dev/docs/

Full Stack Vault - Nov 21

Once unsuspended, schreddies will be able to comment and publish posts again.
